message:
fix(process): kill_on_drop background reaping prevents zombie processes when runtime is available

Command::kill_on_drop(true) previously sent SIGKILL on drop but
did not reap the child, leaving a zombie process.

Fix: New reap_kill_on_drop_child() kills and waits for the child.
should_background_reap_kill_on_drop() checks for a current Cx or
Runtime handle — when available, the reap runs as a background task
on the runtime; when not (e.g. in tests without a runtime), it
performs a synchronous blocking wait.

This ensures that all kill_on_drop children are properly reaped
regardless of the execution context.

Tests:
- test_command_kill_on_drop_reaps_process (unix): spawns sleep(100)
  with kill_on_drop, drops the child, verifies the process is no
  longer alive via kill(pid, 0).
- test_async_kill_on_drop_reaps_process (unix): same but with an
  async runtime context, verifying the background reap path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
